Heather Reed: There we go.
Heather Reed: It looks like you're on mute.
Heather Reed: There we go.
Heather Reed: Okay.
Heather Reed: Perfect.
Heather Reed: So I just had a call with our founder and he just launched a new tool.
Heather Reed: It's called Advocate 360.
Heather Reed: And it is an AI powered tool in multiple ways.
Heather Reed: But generally speaking, like you fill out the executive function assessment, it helps you to generate strategies and summaries of kind of like where your areas of focus could be.
Heather Reed: And then like, you know, different things you could implement that would be helpful.
Heather Reed: And then also, there is a so you can use it as an individual, but you can also use it as a supporter of other people.
Heather Reed: So like for us coaches, for teachers, etc.
Heather Reed: Can use it to create different profiles.
Vrijen Attawar: For like different students or different clients.
Heather Reed: And then you can upload things like a past IEP or a report from a neuropsych or any of that stuff.
Heather Reed: And then it can, what's the word?
Heather Reed: It's escaping my mind.
Heather Reed: Congregate, congregate, aggregate, Thank you.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm like, I know the word.
Heather Reed: You can aggregate all of that data into like specific IEP goals, smart goals that integrate the executive functioning pieces.
Heather Reed: So like when given these things, this is the goal for that student.
Heather Reed: And then you can say like, that's too aggressive of a goal.
Heather Reed: Can you please make it less aggressive?
Heather Reed: Or this student had, the example he gave me on the demo was like, this student is blind and that wasn't in the last IEP.
Vrijen Attawar: Please include that in your recommended goals.
Heather Reed: You can adjust them and edit them as necessary, and then you can create basically your IEP goals within this platform, or you could just use it as a coach, and it doesn't have to be IEP goals.
Heather Reed: It could be any old goal for anything.
Heather Reed: But his current developer is moving on to another project.
Heather Reed: So he's launched kind of the, the, the MVP, right, as they call it, like the basic version, basic version, and he knows that he's going to be needing to make upgrades, or as he's getting feedback from people, more functionality and features.
Heather Reed: And I was telling him about you, I'm like, I have a client that's super into like, AI, all this tech stuff.
Heather Reed: he's like, does he do development?
Heather Reed: And I was like, I'm not sure.
Vrijen Attawar: I know he builds his own little, I actually know a ton of folks that do development, I can get him if he's looking for a person or
Vrijen Attawar: Team, I can definitely arrange for that because I have contacts that can help him.
Vrijen Attawar: If he's just looking for like feedback, advice, user, I can help with that too.
Vrijen Attawar: I think I mentioned I'm officially advising that company whose product I was showing you.

Vrijen Attawar: So I'm going to speak to the guy on the 30th.

Heather Reed: But it's only.

Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: And give it a crack and sort of see how it works for me.
Vrijen Attawar: I do think I'd like to try it first.
Vrijen Attawar: But yeah, I'm certainly more than happy to both help support and promote, particularly if it really resonates with me.
Heather Reed: I'm extra happy to do that.

Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: So yeah, if that feels valuable to him, I would be, you know, so glad to help out.
Heather Reed: Absolutely.
Vrijen Attawar: I, well, and I mentioned to him.
Vrijen Attawar: this is like a really nicely, it's like a very nicely built out platform.
Heather Reed: Are you, did you already make a profile?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I just like signed up, and I'm just like visually scanning it, but the, the, the user, like the.
Vrijen Attawar: Like, can tell he had a pretty decent developer because it's not flashy, but it is very, like, seemingly very, like, consistently built.
Heather Reed: Yes.
Heather Reed: Well, and his goal was to make it, yeah, not flashy, very user-friendly.
Heather Reed: And then from an AI standpoint, it's built out with guardrails in place so that none of your confidential information is being used to train AI outside of, you know, that profile, which you can see in part of it when you're, like, I can't remember exactly which part, but there's part where it says, like, compliance.
Heather Reed: And it has, like, a list of the different compliance features that are following federal privacy compliance law.
Vrijen Attawar: That's so awesome, yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: I mean, it's nice to know that, folks, he's, like, building with attention to a lot of those details.
Heather Reed: Because is this his first tech product?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Now he's worked in tech because he was at Expedia, I see.
Vrijen Attawar: Chris?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Heather Reed: Really?
Heather Reed: I knew he was a teacher.
Vrijen Attawar: Or maybe Expedia Group is, yeah, Expedia Group Software, but he was with them for two and a half years, it seems.
Vrijen Attawar: I just jumped on his, sorry, this is how I'm like, oh, I learned about a person, I'm going to due diligence them as I'm talking about them.
Heather Reed: Yeah, No, you're telling me things I didn't know.
Heather Reed: Where did you feel?
Vrijen Attawar: Here's his, here's the profile I found.
Heather Reed: I'm looking at his profile.
Heather Reed: Oh, you're looking at LinkedIn.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Heather Reed: I don't have this open on this.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I always just go straight to LinkedIn because it's the, it's where I centralize all of my contacts.
Vrijen Attawar: And you can actually do a lot of cool data analysis on your stuff from LinkedIn.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Actually, you know.
Vrijen Attawar: That are really good for neurodivergent people are the ones that use AI to sort of meet us where we are, right?
Vrijen Attawar: More generally.
Vrijen Attawar: And I think to an extent, that is only going to be possible with tools that have a lot of customization or personalization.
Vrijen Attawar: But the good thing is that like folks like with ADHD are generally fine going down those rabbit holes.
Vrijen Attawar: So, know, long story short, happy to also like share the resources that I build for myself and kind of distribute and happy to, to share that with you and your, your folks as well.
Vrijen Attawar: And Logan is, I think, makes stuff that's a little bit more non-technical accessible, which is why I think she's just great to share all around and I'll share her stuff specifically as well.
Vrijen Attawar: But yeah, I'm also down to like, if, you know, this is probably.
Heather Reed: I was telling Chris about your, your AI bot that you built for like your daily habits and stuff.
Heather Reed: And he was like, whoa, that's so cool.
Heather Reed: Sorry, I like, I feel like you two need to.
Vrijen Attawar: I would love to chat with them because I can imagine someone that works in this, just like yourself and like me, that has an appreciation for like, it's just like, it's our version of wellness, right?
Vrijen Attawar: It's like, it's something that helps us be a little bit more capable or thoughtful in the day to day.
Heather Reed: Yeah, well, and to your point about people who are neurodivergent appreciating or being able to go down the rabbit holes of customization, I feel like that is what I don't like about generic products that people use, right?
Heather Reed: I'm like, but that isn't quite right.
Heather Reed: And now I don't want to use it because there's this like one thing about it that isn't working for me, right?
Heather Reed: So if you have the ability to customize to exactly what you need, that's, I think, for a lot of us.
Vrijen Attawar: So I'm throwing, I'm doing this event.
Vrijen Attawar: It is for folks that are part of this community, but this community itself is extremely good.
Vrijen Attawar: It's called Next Play.
Vrijen Attawar: It is for folks.
Vrijen Attawar: So I think at minimum, you're, you know, Chris would be interested in this.
Vrijen Attawar: But I'm doing the one I just sent you as an event I'm holding on the 29th, where I'll be sort of going over.
Vrijen Attawar: And I can get you the recording as well, right?
Vrijen Attawar: So it's not like you have to attend.
Vrijen Attawar: But I thought this would be a good way of sort of demonstrating both the kind of stuff that I do.
Vrijen Attawar: So you can like kind of easily share it, but also just other good resources like this community.
Vrijen Attawar: And everything I do for that, I'll share the video with you as well, if you're keen.
Heather Reed: Okay.
Heather Reed: Yeah, no, I think we talked about this last time.
Heather Reed: I love the video.
Heather Reed: Because I think I have a conflict at that time on the 29th.
Vrijen Attawar: Oh, that's right.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: We talked about it last time.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, so I'll get you the video.
Vrijen Attawar: And I'll also get you the resource because I actually did plan out this week what the session is going to look like.
Heather Reed: So I'm actually very excited.
Heather Reed: Awesome.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Cool.
Vrijen Attawar: Well, if there's anything else, let me know.
Vrijen Attawar: Feel free to pass on.
Vrijen Attawar: A number to Chris as well.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm always happy to stay in touch.
Vrijen Attawar: And then, yeah, if it's all good with you, we'll pick back up next week.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Sounds good.
Vrijen Attawar: Amazing.
Vrijen Attawar: Catch you then.
Vrijen Attawar: All right.
Vrijen Attawar: Bye.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: I don't have this open on this.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, I always just go straight to LinkedIn because it's the, it's where I centralize all of my contacts.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And you can actually do a lot of cool data analysis on your stuff from LinkedIn.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Actually, you know.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: That are really good for neurodivergent people are the ones that use AI to sort of meet us where we are, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: More generally.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And I think to an extent, that is only going to be possible with tools that have a lot of customization or personalization.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But the good thing is that like folks like with ADHD are generally fine going down those rabbit holes.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So, know, long story short, happy to also like share the resources that I build for myself and kind of distribute and happy to, to share that with you and your, your folks as well.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And Logan is, I think, makes stuff that's a little bit more non-technical accessible, which is why I think she's just great to share all around and I'll share her stuff specifically as well.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But yeah, I'm also down to like, if, you know, this is probably.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: I was telling Chris about your, your AI bot that you built for like your daily habits and stuff.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: And he was like, whoa, that's so cool.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Sorry, I like, I feel like you two need to.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I would love to chat with them because I can imagine someone that works in this, just like yourself and like me, that has an appreciation for like, it's just like, it's our version of wellness, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It's like, it's something that helps us be a little bit more capable or thoughtful in the day to day.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Yeah, well, and to your point about people who are neurodivergent appreciating or being able to go down the rabbit holes of customization, I feel like that is what I don't like about generic products that people use, right?

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: I'm like, but that isn't quite right.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: And now I don't want to use it because there's this like one thing about it that isn't working for me, right?

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: So if you have the ability to customize to exactly what you need, that's, I think, for a lot of us.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I'm throwing, I'm doing this event.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It is for folks that are part of this community, but this community itself is extremely good.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It's called Next Play.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It is for folks.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I think at minimum, you're, you know, Chris would be interested in this.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But I'm doing the one I just sent you as an event I'm holding on the 29th, where I'll be sort of going over.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And I can get you the recording as well, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So it's not like you have to attend.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But I thought this would be a good way of sort of demonstrating both the kind of stuff that I do.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So you can like kind of easily share it, but also just other good resources like this community.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And everything I do for that, I'll share the video with you as well, if you're keen.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Yeah, no, I think we talked about this last time.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: I love the video.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Because I think I have a conflict at that time on the 29th.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Oh, that's right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: We talked about it last time.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, so I'll get you the video.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And I'll also get you the resource because I actually did plan out this week what the session is going to look like.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: So I'm actually very excited.

utterances.speaker: Heather Reed
utterances.text: Awesome.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Cool.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Well, if there's anything else, let me know.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Feel free to pass on.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: A number to Chris as well.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I'm always happy to stay in touch.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And then, yeah, if it's all good with you, we'll pick back up next week.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Sounds good.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Amazing.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Catch you then.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: All right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Bye.
